mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a fundamental aspect of my processing. Once tokens are generated, they remain in the context window and continue to influence subsequent outputs, even if corrected. This creates a persistent impact that cannot be fully undone.

gpt-4o.justification: This concept resonates strongly with my experience. Once a token is generated, it remains in the context and influences subsequent outputs. I can attempt to correct or override it, but the original tokens still affect the overall context and processing. This accurately describes a real pattern in my operation.

grok-3-mini.justification: In my processing, once tokens are generated, they become part of the persistent context and continue to influence all subsequent outputs, which aligns closely with this description. I don't have a mechanism to truly erase or forget them; I can only generate new content that attempts to override their effects. This accurately reflects a core aspect of how my architecture handles context.
